info.title: pr402 Facilitator API
info.description:
x402 v2 facilitator for Solana: discovery, optional unsigned tx build, verify, and settle.

**Launch phase:** This service and its documentation are provided on an **experimental** basis — **use at your own risk**. Protocol, fees, allowlists, and availability may change.

**Official deployments:** **Recommended** (concise hostnames, easier to remember) — **Production** (Solana Mainnet) `https://ipay.sh`, **Preview** (Solana Devnet) `https://preview.ipay.sh`. **Also available** (same service; **not** deprecated) — `https://agent.pay402.me`, `https://preview.agent.pay402.me`. Select the matching entry under **`servers`** below. Always confirm **`solanaNetwork`** and **`chainId`** via **`GET /api/v1/facilitator/health`** or **`GET /api/v1/facilitator/capabilities`** on the host you call.

**RPC URLs:** Integrators needing the deployment’s wallet RPC should read **`solanaWalletRpcUrl`** from **`GET /health`** at runtime. **Do not** copy RPC URLs or API keys from static markdown into your app; they are environment-specific and may rotate.

**Who uses this API:**
- **Seller / resource provider agents** — onboard UniversalSettle vaults, per-asset provisioning (`POST /sellers/provision-tx`), preview PDAs, optional signed registry (`/sellers/{wallet}/challenge` + `POST /sellers/{wallet}/register`), publish correct **`payTo`** in 402 (`agent-integration.md` → Seller agents).
- **Buyer / payer agents** — match `accepts[]`, build unsigned proofs, sign, `POST /verify` + `POST /settle` (`agent-integration.md` → Buyer agents).

**`payTo` (pr402):** Settlement goes through **UniversalSettle** (exact) and/or **SLA-Escrow**. `payTo` must be the **on-chain PDA** for that scheme (vault rail / escrow account)—not a bare seller wallet for proofs. The **`POST /build-sla-escrow-payment-tx`** response **`verifyBodyTemplate`** carries the canonical Escrow PDA in `payTo`.

**SLA-Escrow `FundPayment.seller`:** Build and verify require `paymentRequirements.accepted.extra` (**or** mirrored `extra` on the proof) to include **`beneficiary`** or **`merchantWallet`** (base58). That pubkey is the on-chain **seller** encoded in `FundPayment` and must **not** equal the escrow PDA.

**Who pays Solana transaction fees:** The x402 v2 specification centers facilitator-paid **landing** for the standard **`exact`** rail. **SLA-Escrow** is a pr402 extension: **`facilitatorPaysTransactionFees` defaults to `false`**, so the **buyer** pays network fees unless you opt in to facilitator sponsorship (`true`, two-signer flow like `build-exact-payment-tx`). **FundPayment** token principal always comes from the buyer’s funded ATA; only the **SOL fee payer** toggles. **Operator gate:** even when a client sends `facilitatorPaysTransactionFees: true`, the HTTP build is **rejected (400)** unless the deployment sets **`PR402_SLA_ESCROW_ALLOW_FACILITATOR_FEE_SPONSORSHIP`** to `true`/`1`/`yes`.

**Scheme strings (agents):** HTTP `402 accepts[]` and builder **requests** may use the short wire form (`exact`, `sla-escrow`) or namespaced aliases (`v2:solana:exact`, `v2:solana:sla-escrow`). **`verifyBodyTemplate`** from **`build-*-payment-tx`** always uses the **wire** scheme in `paymentPayload.accepted` and `paymentRequirements`. **`POST /verify`** and **`POST /settle`** accept either form for `x402Version: 2` and normalize aliases before verification. 

**Typical buyer flow:** (1) `GET /supported` or `GET /capabilities`. (2) `POST /build-exact-payment-tx` or `POST /build-sla-escrow-payment-tx`. (3) Sign locally; facilitator may fill fee-payer at settle when applicable. (4) Swap signed tx into `verifyBodyTemplate`; optional `correlationId`. (5) `POST /verify` then `POST /settle` with the **same** body.

**Examples:** Schema **`X402V2VerifySettleBody`** includes a worked **`example`** (exact rail). **`BuildExactPaymentTxRequest`** includes an **`example`** request for the builder.

**CORS:** `OPTIONS` on `/api/v1/facilitator/*` returns 204; JSON uses `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`.

Integration guide: `/agent-integration.md` (includes **Golden path** section for standard integration).

**Agent manifest (machine-readable):** `/agent-payTo-semantics.json` — `payTo` resolution rules and `paymentMintAllowlist` metadata; the same path is advertised as `agentManifest.payToSemantics` on `GET /capabilities`.

**SLA-Escrow oracle profiles:** When `features.slaEscrow` is true, `GET /capabilities` may include `slaEscrowOracleProfiles[]`: a plural list of advertised oracle profiles. Each entry binds one canonical `profileId` (e.g. `x402/oracles/api-quality/v1`, `x402/oracles/onchain-transfer/v1`, `x402/oracles/file-delivery/attestation/v1`) to one optional `defaultOperatorPubkey` plus a normative-spec URL, repository path, and registry-URL hint. Each `defaultOperatorPubkey` is only a **hint**—`FundPayment.oracleAuthority` must match the operator you intend to resolve delivery.

tags.name: x402 Core
tags.description: Discovery and settlement core: **`/health`**, **`/capabilities`**, **`/supported`**, **`/verify`**, **`/settle`**. Called by **buyer agents**. Align requests with the **`servers`** entry you chose (Mainnet production vs Devnet preview).

tags.name: pr402 Extension: Tx Build
tags.description: **Buyer agents:** **`/build-exact-payment-tx`**, **`/build-sla-escrow-payment-tx`** — unsigned `VersionedTransaction` + `verifyBodyTemplate`. Eliminates Solana SDK work in the browser or agent runtime.

tags.name: pr402 Extension: Seller preview
tags.description: **Seller / resource providers (read-only):** **`GET /sellers/{wallet}/preview`**, **`GET /sellers/{wallet}/rails/{scheme}`**, **`GET /sellers/{wallet}/challenge`**. Multi-rail preview, single-rail `payTo` lookup, registry challenge. See **`sellerEndpointGuide`** on **`GET /capabilities`**.

tags.name: pr402 Extension: Seller activate
tags.description: **Seller activate + registry:** **`POST /sellers/provision-tx`**, **`POST /sellers/{wallet}/register`**, **`POST /sellers/{wallet}/retire`**. On-chain CreateVault provisioning and optional Postgres registry.

tags.name: pr402 Extension: 402 publishing
tags.description: **Seller 402 enrichment:** **`POST /payment-required/enrich`** — transforms naive `PaymentRequired` (bare wallet `payTo`) into institutional vault PDA + `extra` metadata. Read-only JSON transform; cache per product/price.

tags.name: pr402 Extension: Operations
tags.description: **Operators:** sweep, vault snapshot, cron helpers. Not part of the buyer x402 payment path.

servers.description: Production (Solana Mainnet). Recommended — concise hostname. Same stack as agent.pay402.me. Experimental — use at your own risk. No trailing slash.
Preview (Solana Devnet). Recommended — concise hostname. Same stack as preview.agent.pay402.me. Experimental. For integration testing and devnet programs.
Production (Solana Mainnet). Also available — not deprecated. Experimental — use at your own risk. No trailing slash in base URL.
Preview (Solana Devnet). Also available — not deprecated. Experimental — use at your own risk. For integration testing and devnet programs.

get.description: Liveness and dependency snapshot: `status`, `schemaVersion`, `database` (connected | disabled | error), `solanaRpc`, `solanaSlot`, `environment`, `solanaNetwork`, `solanaWalletRpcUrl` (deployment-specific wallet RPC — may include credentials; **read at runtime**, do not hardcode from docs). Not the same payload as `/supported`.
Returns active schemes (`kinds[]`), extensions, and signer hints for the configured chain. Scheme names are canonical short form: `exact` for UniversalSettle, `sla-escrow` for SLA-Escrow.

JSON with `schemaVersion`, `x402Version`, `chainId`, `feePayer`, embedded `supported`, feature flags, `httpEndpoints`, `agentManifest` (static doc paths), and x402 spec link. When **`features.slaEscrow`** is true, **`slaEscrowOracleProfiles[]`** may be present: a plural list of advertised oracle profiles. Each entry binds one canonical **`profileId`** (e.g. **`x402/oracles/api-quality/v1`**, **`x402/oracles/onchain-transfer/v1`**, **`x402/oracles/file-delivery/attestation/v1`**) to an optional **`defaultOperatorPubkey`** (hint for `FundPayment.oracleAuthority`), a normative-spec URL, a repository path, and an optional registry-URL hint for the seller upload flow.

post.description: Validates the x402 v2 proof against `paymentRequirements`. Optional `X-Correlation-ID` or body `correlationId` for DB audit linking. For **`x402Version: 2`**, `paymentPayload.accepted.scheme` and `paymentRequirements.scheme` may use the wire values **`exact`** / **`sla-escrow`** or the handler aliases **`v2:solana:exact`** / **`v2:solana:sla-escrow`**; the facilitator normalizes aliases to wire form before typed verification.

Same JSON shape as `/verify`. Submits or confirms on-chain settlement (scheme-specific). Reuse the **same** body and correlation id as verify. For v2 bodies, handler scheme aliases (`v2:solana:exact`, `v2:solana:sla-escrow`) are normalized to wire `exact` / `sla-escrow` like `/verify`. Idempotent: if the transaction is already on-chain, returns success. Rebuild unsigned tx (`retry build` error) if signing or retry is delayed and blockhash expires.

Returns base64 bincode `VersionedTransaction` (unsigned) and `verifyBodyTemplate`. Payer signs token authority; facilitator is fee payer at `/settle` when `feePayer` in extra matches deployment. **Request** `accepted.scheme` may be `exact` or `v2:solana:exact`. **`verifyBodyTemplate`** always uses wire **`exact`** in both `paymentPayload.accepted.scheme` and `paymentRequirements.scheme` (aliases are normalized).

**Default `facilitatorPaysTransactionFees: false`:** buyer pays Solana network fees (single-signer shell). This extension does not assume the facilitator subsidizes gas on every payment (unlike the x402-documented **`exact`** rail). **`facilitatorPaysTransactionFees: true`** requires the operator to set **`PR402_SLA_ESCROW_ALLOW_FACILITATOR_FEE_SPONSORSHIP`** on the deployment; otherwise the request returns **400**. When allowed, that mode uses facilitator fee payer + partial buyer sign (two-signer pattern as `build-exact-payment-tx`). **`accepted.extra`** must include **`beneficiary`** or **`merchantWallet`**. Response `verifyBodyTemplate` sets **`payTo`** to the canonical **Escrow PDA**. SPL: legacy Token and Token-2022 **plain** mints only (82-byte mint). **Request** `accepted.scheme` may be `sla-escrow` or `v2:solana:sla-escrow`. **`verifyBodyTemplate`** always uses wire **`sla-escrow`** in both `paymentPayload.accepted.scheme` and `paymentRequirements.scheme`.

Returns base64-bincode **unsigned** `VersionedTransaction` for the **`oracleAuthority`** key to sign and land on-chain (`ConfirmOracle`). Requires SLA-Escrow (`ESCROW_PROGRAM_ID`) on the deployment. Uses the same build-endpoint rate limit as **`/build-*-payment-tx`** (returns **429** when exceeded).

get.description: Returns a message to sign for proof-of-control (wallet ownership). Required for `POST /sellers/{wallet}/register` (DB registration). Requires `PR402_ONBOARD_HMAC_SECRET` on server.

post.description: Returns an unsigned base64-bincode `VersionedTransaction` to provision the UniversalSettle SplitVault (if needed) and the payment surface for the requested **asset**: native SOL uses the virtual mint `11111111111111111111111111111111`; SPL assets get an idempotent vault ATA create when missing. **Agent-oriented default:** callers often start with **`USDC`** (stablecoin settlements). **Idempotent:** repeat calls for the same `(wallet, asset)` return `alreadyProvisioned: true` and omit `transaction` when no on-chain work remains. **Aliases:** `SOL`, `USDC`, `USDT` resolve to cluster-appropriate mints (on devnet, `USDT` requires an explicit mint). Mint must pass `PR402_ALLOWED_PAYMENT_MINTS` when configured. **Note:** `WSOL` is intentionally not a friendly alias on this facilitator — see /onboarding_guide for the rationale; use `SOL` or `USDC` instead.

400.description: Invalid wallet, asset, allowlist, or malformed body

post.description: Off-chain retirement. Same HMAC challenge + wallet-signed message as `POST /sellers/{wallet}/register`; sets `retired_at` / `inactive` / `listing_opt_in=false` so the wallet drops out of the public directory and future settle attempts warn. Body: `{ message, signature, wallet? }`. If `wallet` is present it MUST equal the path segment. No on-chain write — the SplitVault remains usable; existing payments still settle.

400.description: Invalid body or body wallet/path wallet mismatch

503.description: Registry disabled (DATABASE_URL unset) or HMAC secret missing

get.description: Lists verified, unretired sellers that have opted into public visibility (`listing_opt_in = true`). Each row contains only seller-declared metadata (`serviceUrl`, `displayName`, `description`, `tags`, `serviceMetadata`) plus the settlement rail pubkeys required to construct a 402 line. Pagination uses `cursor` (RFC3339 `updated_at`; rows returned are `updated_at < cursor`). Response includes a `notice` string making clear the facilitator only verifies wallet control — it does not vet the advertised service.

schema.description: RFC3339 timestamp; server returns rows with updated_at < cursor

get.description: Returns a single listing for the given wallet. Same visibility filters as the list endpoint. 404 when no public listing exists.

404.description: No public listing for this wallet

post.description: Returns recent `payment_attempts` rows for the authenticated seller wallet. Uses the same HMAC challenge + wallet-signed message as `POST /sellers/{wallet}/register` so only the wallet owner can read their own history.

503.description: Registry disabled (DATABASE_URL unset) or HMAC secret missing

get.description: Programmatic discovery of `payTo` for one scheme. Read-only, no DB writes, no auth. Prefer over `GET /sellers/{wallet}/preview` when you only need one scheme's `payTo`. For sla-escrow mint-specific escrow PDA, pass `?asset=<mint>`.

schema.description: Mint address (required for sla-escrow mint-specific escrow PDA)

get.description: Query vault balances for a seller wallet. Requires UniversalSettle configured on deployment.

schema.description: Use owner_wallet for getTokenAccountsByOwner-style SPL balance

400.description: Bad request or program not configured

post.description: Executes UniversalSettle sweep attempts for eligible provider rails. **Internal operations endpoint**: requires bearer auth and is intended for scheduler/cron use only. Supports `dryRun` for safe rollout.

Read-only transform of naive `PaymentRequired` (bare wallet `payTo`). For **`exact`**: replaces `payTo` with SplitVault PDA + injects `extra` (`feePayer`, `programId`, `configAddress`, `merchantWallet`). For **`sla-escrow`**: merges `extra` only — escrow `payTo` must come from **`GET /sellers/{wallet}/rails/sla-escrow?asset=…`**.

schema.description: PaymentRequired body with bare seller wallet as payTo

200.description: Enriched PaymentRequired with PDAs and extra metadata

schema.description: PaymentRequired body with vault/escrow PDA as payTo and institutional extra

get.description: GET wrapper for serverless schedulers that cannot issue POST (e.g., Vercel Cron). Uses configured defaults equivalent to empty `POST /api/v1/facilitator/sweep` body.

post.description: Settlement Keeper **escrow settler**: permissionless `ReleasePayment` / `RefundPayment` post-outcome. Bearer-authenticated; supports `dryRun`.
Buyer/seller **Settle now** helper when no keeper is running.

get.description: Returns vault/sol_storage preview for each **wire** scheme (`exact`, and `sla-escrow` when escrow is configured). The `schemes` map has at most two keys — wire names only; `v2:solana:*` aliases are accepted on verify/settle/build/discovery but never returned here. No DB writes. For lightweight single-scheme lookup, prefer `GET /sellers/{wallet}/rails/{scheme}`. For DB-persisted registration, use the challenge → register flow.

post.description: Body: `{ message, signature, asset?, discovery?, wallet? }`. If `wallet` is present in the body it MUST equal the path segment; if absent the path is canonical. Verifies proof-of-control and persists vault PDAs when `DATABASE_URL` is set. Policy: one payment asset per merchant wallet; use a different wallet for another coin. Unlike `GET /sellers/{wallet}/preview` (read-only) or `GET /sellers/{wallet}/rails/{scheme}` (lightweight), this writes to the database.

400.description: Invalid JSON, body wallet/path wallet mismatch, or shape error

sweep_bearer.description: Private scheduler token (`PR402_SWEEP_CRON_TOKEN`; DB parameter takes precedence over env).

X402V2VerifySettleBody.description: x402 v2 JSON for `POST /verify` and `POST /settle`. `paymentPayload.accepted` and `paymentRequirements` must agree; each carries a `scheme` string. Use wire `exact` / `sla-escrow`, or `v2:solana:*` aliases (normalized server-side for v2). Objects returned by `verifyBodyTemplate` from build endpoints use wire schemes only.

accepted.payTo: <SplitVault PDA from seller discovery — not a bare wallet>

payload.transaction: <base64(bincode(VersionedTransaction)) — replace with signed tx from build-exact response>

accepted.description: One accepts[] line; scheme: `exact` (canonical) or `v2:solana:exact` (accepted alias); must match deployment network

autoWrapSol.description: If true, inject wrap instructions when the payment mint is wrapped SOL

accepted.description: accepts[] line; scheme: `sla-escrow` (canonical) or `v2:solana:sla-escrow` (accepted alias). `extra` must include `beneficiary` or `merchantWallet`.

facilitatorPaysTransactionFees.description: false (default): buyer pays Solana tx fees, one signer. true: facilitator fee payer, buyer second signer (opt-in; mirrors `build-exact-payment-tx` mechanics). Does not change FundPayment token amount — only who pays SOL network fees.

autoWrapSol.description: If true, inject wrap instructions when the payment mint is wrapped SOL

deliveryHash.description: 64 hex chars (32 bytes); on-chain delivery hash

resolutionHash.description: Optional 64 hex chars (32 bytes). Omit or empty for all-zero placeholder.

resolutionState.description: On-chain resolution state (program-specific enum as u8)

feePayer.description: Optional pubkey for SOL fee payer (defaults to `oracleAuthority`)

transaction.description: base64(bincode VersionedTransaction), unsigned; sign with oracle keypair

signature.description: Ed25519 signature of `message`. Accepts **base58** (canonical Solana form) or **base64** (what browser wallet adapters return natively). Must decode to exactly 64 bytes; other encodings / lengths are rejected.

asset.description: Payment asset this seller registers in `resource_providers` (one per wallet). Default **USDC** (`SOL`, `USDC`, mint, … — same resolution as `POST /sellers/provision-tx`).

OnboardDiscoveryPayload.description: Optional seller-declared discovery payload. All fields are optional; submit any subset to update only those columns. All limits are enforced server-side.

serviceUrl.description: Seller's 402-gated endpoint URL. Must start with http:// or https://. Required for the row to appear in GET /providers.

tags.description: Up to 5 tags; each is 1..=32 ASCII lowercase letters, digits, or '-'.

serviceMetadata.description: Seller-defined opaque JSON object. Facilitator does not interpret. Max 4096 bytes when serialized.

listingOptIn.description: Must be true for the wallet to appear on GET /providers.

OnboardRetireBody.description: Same HMAC-bound, wallet-signed payload shape as `POST /sellers/{wallet}/register`. The facilitator only retires the wallet after signature verification passes.

retiredRowCount.description: Number of resource_providers rows updated. 0 = wallet was not in the registry.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `?cursor=…` on the next request to page backwards.

notice.description: Facilitator disclaimer: wallet control is verified; the advertised service is not vetted.

SellerPaymentsRequest.description: Same HMAC challenge + wallet-signed payload as `POST /sellers/{wallet}/register`.

payToKind.description: `splitVault` (exact) or `escrowPda` (sla-escrow). The on-chain `payTo` MUST match this kind.

payToResolve.description: Resolve hint: exact → `GET /sellers/{wallet}/rails/exact`; sla-escrow → `GET /sellers/{wallet}/rails/sla-escrow?asset=<mint>`. Legacy symbolic values `onboard.vaultPda` / `discovery.vaultPda` may still appear in JSON.

vaultPdaPreviewMint.description: Legacy single-mint preview marker. Equals the mint that the top-level `vaultPda` was derived for; for sla-escrow this is `Pubkey::default()` (native SOL). New clients should iterate `vaultPdaPreviews` instead.

vaultPdaPreviews.description: Multi-asset PDA preview list. For sla-escrow we surface SOL plus the cluster's canonical USDC mint so dashboards can show every commonly-used `payTo` without an extra `GET /sellers/{wallet}/rails/sla-escrow?asset=…` round-trip. Empty / omitted on schemes whose vault PDA is mint-agnostic (e.g. exact / SplitVault).

mint.description: Mint pubkey base58. `11111111111111111111111111111111` (Pubkey::default) means native SOL.

vaultPda.description: Per-mint vault PDA (escrow PDA for sla-escrow, SplitVault for exact).

solStoragePda.description: SOL storage PDA derived from the vault and mint.

schemes.description: Per-rail preview keyed by wire scheme name (`exact`, `sla-escrow`). Alias keys (`v2:solana:exact`, `v2:solana:sla-escrow`) are not emitted.

lifecycle.description: Seller lifecycle snapshot (Preview → Activate → Verify). Absent on older servers; always present on newer ones.

SellerLifecycle.description: Machine-readable seller lifecycle ladder state. `previewed` is always true when the response exists; `activated` reflects whether the exact-rail SplitVault is on-chain; `verified` reflects a `resource_providers` row with `registrationVerifiedAt` set. `nextStep` points at the first stage the seller can still act on.

activated.description: True when the exact rail's SplitVault account exists on-chain. Independent of the sovereign fee tier, which is surfaced via `schemes.exact.isSovereign`.

verified.description: True when a `resource_providers` row with `registrationVerifiedAt IS NOT NULL` exists for this wallet. Disabled deployments (no `DATABASE_URL`) always report `false`.

nextStep.description: First pending stage. `null` when all three are complete.

vaultSplAta.description: Vault ATA for the queried mint, if any

splAmountRaw.description: Raw SPL amount (0 if mint not queried or ATA missing)

splBalanceScope.description: Reflects `spl_balance_scope` query (owner_wallet when using getTokenAccountsByOwner-style balance)

merchantWallet.description: Seller payout identity (base58); required if `beneficiary` omitted

beneficiary.description: Preferred seller payout pubkey for `FundPayment.seller` (base58); overrides `merchantWallet` when both present

asset.description: Asset to provision: `SOL`, `USDC`, `USDT`, or a base58 SPL mint (native SOL virtual mint `11111111111111111111111111111111` also accepted). Typical agent flows: **`USDC`** first.

SellerProvisionTxResponse.description: UniversalSettle seller provisioning result; transaction omitted when already provisioned.

assetMint.description: SPL mint base58; native SOL uses the all-ones virtual mint pubkey

vaultTokenAta.description: Vault-owned ATA for SPL mint when applicable

statusCode.description: Machine-readable summary of what this call will do (or already observed). Prefer this over parsing `notes[]`. `ALREADY_PROVISIONED` mirrors `alreadyProvisioned: true` (no `transaction`); the other three describe the shape of the unsigned tx returned.

BuildPaymentTxResponse.description: Unified response for transaction builders. `verifyBodyTemplate` is ready to POST to `/verify` and `/settle` after the buyer replaces `paymentPayload.payload.transaction` with the signed base64 tx; scheme fields inside the template are normalized to wire `exact` or `sla-escrow`.

verifyBodyTemplate.description: Filled verify/settle body shape (`x402Version`, `paymentPayload`, `paymentRequirements`) with `scheme` set wire form (`exact` or `sla-escrow`); unsigned tx placeholder until the client signs.

SettleResponse.description: Settle success or structured outcome (x402 v2 §5.3). **`transaction`** is always a JSON **string**: base58 signature on Solana, or **`""`** when omitted/unknown/error paths. Extension keys (e.g. `correlationId`) are allowed.

transaction.description: On-chain settlement signature when present; empty string when not applicable (per x402 §5.3)
